title: Passiflora mollissima
taxonomy: Passiflora mollissima is a species of flowering plant in the genus Passiflora. It belongs to the family Passifloraceae, which consists of around 560 species of passionflower plants. The Passifloraceae family is part of the larger order Malpighiales, within the clade Rosids. Passiflora mollissima is native to tropical regions, specifically in Southeast Asia and northern Australia. Its classification is further detailed by its classification at the kingdom level as Plantae.
uses: Passiflora mollissima has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. It is reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of certain health conditions. In some parts of the world, the plant is used to treat fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. The plant's fruit and leaves are often used in herbal remedies, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential health benefits. Further studies are necessary to confirm the efficacy of Passiflora mollissima in treating various ailments.

side_effects: Passiflora mollissima, also known as the sweet granadilla, is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. While there is limited information available on the side effects of Passiflora mollissima, it is known to be potentially toxic and can cause skin irritation in some individuals. Ingesting large quantities of the fruit or its juice may lead to gastrointestinal issues such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Additionally, Passiflora mollissima contains alkaloids that can interact with certain medications, including blood thinners and sedatives. intro: Passiflora mollissima, also known as the yellow granadilla or the hairy passionflower, is a species of flowering plant in the Passifloraceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The health benefits of Passiflora mollissima include its ability to act as an anxiolytic and sedative, helping to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, as well as its potential to alleviate symptoms of insomnia and restlessness. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Passiflora mollissima include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
